title: Eye makeup remover oil
subtitle: A gentle oil-based eye makeup remover with sweet almond, castor, and chamomile essential oil
excerpt: Oil dissolves oil-based makeup far more effectively than water-based cleansers. This formula uses sweet almond oil as the primary solvent, castor oil for slip, a trace of chamomile essential oil for its soothing properties, and rose water for a mild hydrating effect. Shake before each use.
sourceNotes: Oil cleanser formulation from natural cosmetics formulation references. Chamomile essential oil safety for eye area from IFRA guidelines.

recipe.batchNotes: Keep batches small due to the rose water component limiting shelf life.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 3 months. The rose water introduces a small water phase which limits shelf life. Discard if the mixture smells off or shows cloudiness other than after shaking.

glossaryTerms.definition: The practice of using plant oils to dissolve and remove makeup, sebum, and surface impurities. Oil is miscible with the oil-based components of makeup and sebum; water is not. The oil is then removed with a warm, damp cloth.
A thick, viscous oil with a high ricinoleic acid content. In an oil cleanser or makeup remover it provides slip and helps the product glide without dragging on the delicate eye area.

A by-product of rose essential oil distillation, or a hydrosol made by steam-distilling rose petals. It has a mild, soothing effect on skin and a pleasant fragrance. In an oil formula it will separate and must be shaken back into suspension before use.
A delicate essential oil with well-documented anti-inflammatory properties. Used in eye-area products at 0.5% or below due to the sensitivity of the surrounding skin.

content.text: This makeup remover works on the principle of
: oil dissolves oil-based products. Sweet almond oil at 70% does the bulk of the work.
at 20% provides the thick, slip-rich texture that prevents tugging on the delicate eye area.
at 9% adds a mild soothing quality and means the product will separate; shake before each use.
at 0.5% is appropriate for use near the eye area at this concentration. Shelf life is 3 months.

items.prepNote: 9% of formula; will separate; shake before use
0.5% of formula; approximately 20 drops per 100 g

content.text: Weigh all ingredients into the dark glass bottle using a funnel. Cap and shake gently to combine. Label with the date made.

To use: shake the bottle, apply a small amount to a cotton pad, and gently press onto closed eyes for 10 seconds before wiping from the inner corner outward. Rinse the eye area with warm water if preferred.

Store in a cool, dark place. Shelf life is 3 months from date of making. The rose water component means this product has a shorter shelf life than a fully water-free oil cleanser. Shake before every use. Discard if the product develops an off smell or if separation no longer resolves with shaking.
